data.title: China's exports jump 8% in April despite tariff tensions
data.description:
• China's Trade Balance for April, in Chinese Yuan (CNY) terms, came in at CNY689.99 billion, showing a slight cooling off from the previous figure of CNY736.72 billion
  • The difference was largely driven by an 8.1% Y/Y rise in exports, well above market forecasts of a 1.9% gain, despite easing from a 12.4% surge in March, as shipments to the US were dampened by Trump's tariffs.
  • Meanwhile, imports fell by 0.2%, softer than market expectations of a 5.9% decline, following a 4.3% drop in March.
  • The trade surplus with the U.S. narrowed to $20.46B in April from $27.58B in March. For the first four months of the year, the country posted a $368.8B surplus. During the period, the trade surplus with the U.S. reached $97.03B, with exports and imports shrinking 2.5% and 4.7%, respectively.

ETFs: (NYSEARCA:FXI), (NYSEARCA:KWEB), (NYSEARCA:CQQQ), (NASDAQ:MCHI), (NYSEARCA:ASHR), (NYSEARCA:YINN), (NYSE:TDF), (NYSEARCA:CHIQ), (NYSEARCA:GXC), (NYSEARCA:EWH), (NYSEARCA:KBA), (NYSEARCA:YANG), (NASDAQ:CXSE), (NYSE:CAF), (NYSEARCA:CWEB), (NASDAQ:PGJ), (NYSEARCA:KURE).

Currency: (CNY:USD)

data.title: FIGS outlines 7.5%-8.5% adjusted EBITDA margin target for 2025 amid tariff mitigation strategies
data.description:
Earnings Call Insights: FIGS, Inc. (FIGS) Q1 2025

Management View

  • CEO Trina Spear highlighted that Q1 2025 revenues increased 5% year-over-year, reaching $124.9 million, surpassing expectations. Adjusted EBITDA margin of 7.2% exceeded the 5.5%-6% target, reflecting top-line improvements and strategic investments.
  • Spear emphasized the ongoing normalization of scrubwear trends following the COVID-19 overhang, with improved average order value (AOV), which reached a record $119, and increased customer reactivations.
  • Investments in international markets, B2B initiatives, and retail expansion remain a priority, with plans to debut FIGS in Japan later this quarter and South Korea in the second half of 2025.
  • CFO Sarah Oughtred reported that 80% of net revenues were generated from scrubwear, which grew 5%, while non-scrubwear accounted for 20% and increased 4%, driven by strong demand for footwear and underscrubs.

Outlook

  • The company maintained its full-year 2025 net revenue guidance of a low single-digit decline year-over-year, citing uncertainties in consumer demand and promotional reductions.
  • Adjusted EBITDA margin guidance was updated to a range of 7.5%-8.5%, incorporating the impacts of a 10% baseline tariff and mitigation strategies. The tariff impact is expected to phase into financial results in the second half of the year.
  • FIGS plans to continue its focus on international expansion, aiming for meaningful growth in Japan and South Korea by leveraging localized efforts and enhanced engagement strategies.

Financial Results

  • Gross margin for Q1 2025 was 67.6%, contracting by 130 basis points due to higher promotional activity and increased freight expenses.
  • Net cash, cash equivalents, and short-term investments totaled $251.2 million, reflecting a strong balance sheet despite share repurchases and strategic investments.
  • Inventory increased 1% year-over-year to $131.6 million but was down 27% from the peak level in Q1 2023, indicating better inventory management.

Q&A

  • Matt Koranda, ROTH Capital: Questioned the decision not to include pricing adjustments to offset tariff impacts. CFO Oughtred highlighted ongoing cost mitigation measures, including supplier negotiations, while CEO Spear emphasized the importance of maintaining affordability for healthcare professionals as a priority over immediate price increases.
  • Brooke Roach, Goldman Sachs: Asked about the impact of tariffs on the Fit initiative. CEO Spear confirmed the initiative remains on track, unaffected by supply chain adjustments.
  • Rick Patel, Raymond James: Inquired about international performance. CFO Oughtred noted 16% growth in international sales, with strong contributions from markets like Mexico, Europe, and the Middle East.

Sentiment Analysis

  • Analysts expressed mixed sentiments, with concerns over the tariff impact and cautious guidance for 2025. Questions around pricing and demand normalization reflected skepticism about near-term growth catalysts.
  • Management maintained a confident tone, emphasizing strategic investments and the strength of their brand and community engagement.
  • Compared to the previous quarter, management’s tone reflected steady confidence, but analysts appeared more focused on external risks and mitigation strategies.

Quarter-over-Quarter Comparison

  • Q1 2025 saw improved adjusted EBITDA margin performance (7.2% compared to Q4 2024's 13.9%) but faced gross margin pressures due to enhanced promotional activities.
  • Revenue growth in Q1 2025 (5% year-over-year) was consistent with Q4 2024, while AOV reached a record level, showcasing brand strength.
  • Management’s focus shifted more toward mitigating tariff impacts and enhancing operational efficiency compared to broader strategic initiatives discussed in Q4 2024.

Risks and Concerns

  • Tariff impacts on cost of goods sold remain a challenge, with potential incremental pressures in the second half of 2025.
  • Analysts raised concerns about consumer behavior shifts and the potential for reduced demand in international markets, particularly Canada.
  • Management acknowledged ongoing uncertainties in global trade policies and consumer spending patterns, emphasizing the need for disciplined execution and expense management.

Final Takeaway

FIGS reported a solid start to fiscal 2025, with revenue growth and improved customer metrics driving better-than-expected adjusted EBITDA margins. Strategic investments in international expansion, retail presence, and B2B initiatives underscore management’s commitment to long-term growth. However, external risks, including tariff impacts and macroeconomic uncertainties, temper near-term optimism, making disciplined execution critical for sustaining momentum.

Read the full Earnings Call Transcript

data.title: Olo Non-GAAP EPS of $0.08 beats by $0.02, revenue of $80.7M beats by $3.26M
data.description:
• Olo press release (NYSE:OLO): Q1 Non-GAAP EPS of $0.08 beats by $0.02.
  • Revenue of $80.7M (+21.3% Y/Y) beats by $3.26M.
  • Total platform revenue increased 20% year-over-year to $79.2 million.
  • Average revenue per unit increased 12% year-over-year to approximately $911.
  • Dollar-based net revenue retention (NRR) was 111%.
  • Ending active locations increased 8% year-over year to approximately 88,000, up approximately 2,000 from the quarter ended December 31, 2024.

  • As of May 8, 2025, Olo is issuing the following outlook:

    For the second quarter of 2025, Olo expects to report:

    • Revenue in the range of $82.0 million to $82.5 million; and
    • Non-GAAP operating income in the range of $11.5 million to $11.8 million.

  • For fiscal year 2025, Olo expects to report:

    • Revenue in the range of $338.5 million to $340.0 million; and
    • Non-GAAP operating income in the range of $48.6 million to $49.8 million.

data.title: Suzano S.A. reports Q1 results
data.description:
• Suzano S.A. press release (NYSE:SUZ): Q1 net revenue of R$11.6 billion, up 22% on the same quarter last year.
  • Adjusted EBITDA totaled R$4.9 billion, a 7% increase over 1Q24.
  • Net profit totaled R$6.3 billion due to the accounting impact of U.S. dollar denominated debt and hedging operations translating into Brazilian Real.
